author: 0xunclebill
content:
i was investigating why lighthouse banned prysm nodes today. We got some payload attestations from prysm that were not in the ptc 

 it seems like prysm was already having some issues and had advanced a stale state to produce payload attestations. According to claude prysm doesn't do much verification before broadcasting its payload attestation. For example lighthouse runs gossip verification on the payload attestations before broadcasting. I think if prysm were to do something similar it wouldnt have broadcasted those invalid messages and we wouldnt have banned them

claude is even telling me that prysm does make a check on `payloadAttestationCommitteeIndex` but only after the payload attestation is broadcasted?

author: potuz
content: I'm a bit shocked though, did you verify the PTC committee with the blockroot? Or with your head state

author: potuz
content: I'm gonna assume that Prysm is right here, namely perhaps the node was already stuck behind and advanced head across a different shuffling

author: potuz
content: Otherwise we will need to audit that duties path <@884836500037066862>

author: potuz
content: There aren't that many cases in which a reject is granted for a PTC attestation <@358120958726373381> , can you give me an example?

author: 0xunclebill
content:
[REJECT] The message's validator index is within the payload committee in get_ptc(state, data.slot). The state is the head state corresponding to processing the block up to the current slot as determined by the fork choice.

im pretty sure it was this one that caused us to penalize prysm

author: potuz
content: Yes but the problem is what is the state that you're using

author: potuz
content: It should be the state being voted upon advanced

author: potuz
content: Just like the target for attestations

author: potuz
content: If my node didn't see any blocks at all for the last three epochs, it's correctly attesting now and it's on a different shuffling than your head

author: potuz
content: We just ignore these messages, but we don't downscored

author: potuz
content: If you have the example we can check

author: 0xunclebill
content:
ill try to get the exact block root you guys attested to. i dont see a bug with the state we were using

we check if our head matches the block root of the payload attestation, if it is we can use the head state

if not we fetch the state by block root from storage (cache or db) and advance to the correct slot. If we dont have the state we raise an internal error and ignore the message
